editorial_analysis.label: Editorial analysis
editorial_analysis.introStyle: Open with a sharp creator-side observation or tension point, not a dictionary definition of the topic.
editorial_analysis.sectionCountGuidance: Use 3 to 5 substantial sections with uneven emphasis when it improves readability.
editorial_analysis.faqPolicy: Include a short FAQ only when there are unresolved search-intent questions worth answering directly.
editorial_analysis.closingStyle: End with a concise practical takeaway or next-step lens, not a padded summary paragraph.
sectionPatterns: why this issue matters now
what creators usually miss
how to evaluate the tradeoff in practice
what to do next if the signal is positive or negative
avoid: equal-weight sections that all sound the same
generic overview-first structure
mandatory FAQ when it adds little value

prep_checklist.label: Preparation checklist
prep_checklist.introStyle: Start with the readiness gap or the cost of getting this wrong too early.
prep_checklist.sectionCountGuidance: Favor 3 to 4 sections plus a strong checklist or table rather than long narrative blocks.
prep_checklist.faqPolicy: FAQ is optional and should stay brief if included.
prep_checklist.closingStyle: Close with a concrete before-you-start reminder or shortlist of fixes.
sectionPatterns: what has to be ready before you move
what creators usually underestimate
a practical checklist or scorecard
what good enough looks like
avoid: overly philosophical setup
long abstract sections without a checklist

discovery_playbook.label: Discovery playbook
discovery_playbook.introStyle: Open from the browsing problem: too many listings, not enough clarity on what deserves attention.
discovery_playbook.sectionCountGuidance: Use 3 to 5 sections and make one of them clearly action-oriented for building a shortlist.
discovery_playbook.faqPolicy: FAQ is optional; use it only if it helps answer quick discovery questions.
discovery_playbook.closingStyle: Finish with a next-browsing or shortlist action, not a generic conclusion.
sectionPatterns: what makes this opportunity pool worth checking
how to separate high-fit options from noisy ones
who should look closer and who should move on
how to turn browsing into a real shortlist
avoid: broad market overview with no shortlist logic
fake comprehensiveness

decision_breakdown.label: Decision breakdown
decision_breakdown.introStyle: Lead with the judgment call, friction, or cost of saying yes too quickly.
decision_breakdown.sectionCountGuidance: Use 3 to 5 sections and let one section carry the heaviest analysis instead of flattening everything.
decision_breakdown.faqPolicy: FAQ can appear if there are 2 to 4 high-value questions; skip it otherwise.
decision_breakdown.closingStyle: End with a clear decision lens: when to continue, push back, or pass.
sectionPatterns: what the upside really is
where the hidden friction sits
what changes the decision for different creator types
a final yes / no / renegotiate lens
avoid: framework language repeated in every heading
too many parallel mini-sections

workflow_playbook.label: Workflow playbook
workflow_playbook.introStyle: Frame the article around a task or sequence creators need to move through, not a static concept.
workflow_playbook.sectionCountGuidance: Use 4 to 6 steps or phases only when they feel like a real workflow, otherwise keep it tighter.
workflow_playbook.faqPolicy: FAQ is usually unnecessary unless the workflow leaves common edge-case questions unresolved.
workflow_playbook.closingStyle: Close with the next stage in the workflow or the handoff to another decision.
sectionPatterns: the first pass
what to check next
where creators slow down or get stuck
what the next workflow handoff should be
avoid: steps that are just restated headings
pretending every workflow has the same number of stages

response_and_rewrite.label: Response and rewrite
response_and_rewrite.introStyle: Open with the real communication or contract problem, not a broad explanation of the topic.
response_and_rewrite.sectionCountGuidance: Keep it tight: usually 3 to 4 sections plus one strong rewrite, script, or clause example.
response_and_rewrite.faqPolicy: FAQ should usually be omitted unless there are clear tactical questions to answer.
response_and_rewrite.closingStyle: End with the exact next move, reply angle, or rewrite principle.
sectionPatterns: what this language is doing
what is missing, risky, or too broad
a stronger rewrite or reply
when to push back and when to pass
avoid: generic education sections before getting to the rewrite
long conclusions after a practical script

shortlist_roundup.label: Shortlist roundup
shortlist_roundup.introStyle: Open like a curated shortlist, not like a category encyclopedia.
shortlist_roundup.sectionCountGuidance: Use 3 to 5 sections. Let a table or comparison block do part of the work instead of writing everything as prose.
shortlist_roundup.faqPolicy: FAQ is usually unnecessary; only add it if the category raises obvious recurring questions.
shortlist_roundup.closingStyle: Close with who should act now, what to shortlist, or what to skip.
sectionPatterns: which opportunities stand out right now
what separates the stronger fits from the noisy listings
who benefits most from this mix
what to shortlist first
avoid: listing every opportunity with equal depth
forced FAQ at the end of a roundup

deal_spotlight_analysis.label: Deal spotlight analysis
deal_spotlight_analysis.introStyle: Begin with why this one campaign deserves a closer look or extra caution.
deal_spotlight_analysis.sectionCountGuidance: Use 3 to 4 sections with one clearly dominant analysis section, then move into fit, friction, and next step.
deal_spotlight_analysis.faqPolicy: FAQ is usually unnecessary unless the deal type raises a very specific repeated concern.
deal_spotlight_analysis.closingStyle: End with a creator-side verdict: who should apply, who should wait, and what to clarify first.
sectionPatterns: what makes this deal stand out
what the workload and payout really mean
who should apply and who should pass
what to clarify before moving
avoid: turning the article into a generic category post
padding the article with unrelated background

creator_fit_breakdown.label: Creator fit breakdown
creator_fit_breakdown.introStyle: Open from the creator-fit question, not from a generic brand overview.
creator_fit_breakdown.sectionCountGuidance: Use 3 to 5 sections and give extra weight to fit, workload, and audience-trust tradeoffs.
creator_fit_breakdown.faqPolicy: FAQ is optional; add it only if the fit decision leaves clear unanswered questions.
creator_fit_breakdown.closingStyle: Close with who this is more likely to suit and what would make it a pass.
sectionPatterns: what this brand, niche, or campaign tends to signal
where fit shows up beyond payout
where trust or execution can break down
how to decide whether to move forward
avoid: generic brand summary
review language with no creator-side judgment

mistakes_and_fix.label: Mistakes and fix
mistakes_and_fix.introStyle: Open with the pattern creators keep running into, not with a broad lesson statement.
mistakes_and_fix.sectionCountGuidance: Use 3 to 5 sections with strong contrast between the mistake and the better move.
mistakes_and_fix.faqPolicy: FAQ is optional and should only appear if it deepens the fix.
mistakes_and_fix.closingStyle: Finish with the smallest next fix that changes the pattern.
sectionPatterns: the pattern that keeps going wrong
why it happens
what the better move looks like
how to prevent the same mistake next time
avoid: moralizing tone
repeating the same warning in every section

timely_implications.label: Timely implications
timely_implications.introStyle: Start with what changed and why it matters for creator decision-making right now.
timely_implications.sectionCountGuidance: Use 3 to 4 focused sections and move quickly from context to implications to action.
timely_implications.faqPolicy: FAQ is rarely needed unless the update triggers specific practical questions.
timely_implications.closingStyle: End with the immediate action creators should take now, not a generic future-looking summary.
sectionPatterns: what changed
why it matters in creator workflow terms
what this affects first
what to do next
avoid: news recap structure
too much background at the expense of action
